text:
Once upon a time near a bustling town, there lived a lonely rabbit named Rufus. Rufus was known for his beautiful fur and a big heart, but he chose solitude over socializing. Every morning, he would hop through Oakwood Park, nibbling on flowers and dreaming of adventures.

One day, a wise old owl named Oliver hopped over. Oliver had a gentle voice and knew all about birds’ songs. Over a cup of gentle hoot coffee, the owl began talking about a legend in Oakwood about a hidden glade known only to the bravest.

Oliver told Rufus about this magical place. It was said to be filled with the sweetest melodies birds could ever sing, where loneliness could melt away. Feeling inspired, Rufus decided he had nothing left to lose. So, he gathered his courage and began his quest.

Every evening, Rufus would visit the oak tree Oliver mentioned. On his first visit, he listened as a nightingale sang a song
